codeReview.summary: The resolver covers the requested core namespaces and diagnostics for the tested happy-path/negative cases, but it leaves some parsed names unresolved. In particular, trait names in `impl Trait for Type` items and trait names used in generic bounds are never validated, so semantically invalid programs can pass name resolution even though Phase 4 is supposed to resolve type/global namespaces consistently.

issues.description: `ItemTraitImpl` resolution ignores `ti_trait`, so `impl MissingTrait for Article { ... }` succeeds even when the trait name is undefined. The AST explicitly carries `ti_trait` (`lib/ast.mli`), parser tests already support trait impl syntax (`test/test_parser.ml`), but the resolver only resolves `ti_ty` and method bodies. This leaves a parsed global type/trait namespace unresolved in Phase 4.

Generic trait bounds are parsed but never resolved. `type_param.tp_bound` stores names like `Display + Summary` (`lib/ast.mli`), and parser tests cover that syntax (`test/test_parser.ml`), yet `resolve_fn_decl`, `ItemStruct`, `ItemEnum`, `ItemImpl`, `ItemTraitImpl`, and `ItemTrait` only add type parameters and resolve field/param/result types. As a result, undefined trait names inside bounds are silently accepted, which is a missed name-resolution case for declared type/global names.

summary: Reviewed the feature handoff, worker transcript skeleton, compiler-worker skill, architecture/services docs, and commit `e7b64e3`. The implementation correctly adds a resolver pass and strong coverage for functions, structs, enum variants, local bindings, fields, and duplicate definitions, but it does not resolve trait names in trait impls or generic bounds even though those names are already present in the parsed AST. I marked the review as failed because those omissions leave semantically invalid programs accepted by Phase 4 name resolution.
